senses.definition: completely or entirely
examples: The train slowed down and then stopped altogether.
I am not altogether happy with the decision.

senses.definition: used to give a total number or amount
examples: You owe me 68 dollars altogether.
Altogether, we invited fifty people to the party.

senses.definition: used to say that something is true in general, considering everything
examples: Altogether, it was a very enjoyable evening.
Altogether, her results have been quite good this year.

senses.definition: the quality of being complete, unified, or whole
examples: The design has a pleasing altogetherness that makes it memorable.
There is an altogetherness to his argument that is hard to break.

senses.definition: different forms or examples of complete or whole things, used rarely and mainly in creative or humorous writing
examples: The three plans are very different altogethers.
In their music, the old and new styles are strange altogethers.

senses.definition: a musician who plays the alto saxophone or sings the alto part
examples: She is one of the finest jazz altoists of her generation.
The choir hired an experienced altoist for the recording.

senses.definition: musicians who play the alto saxophone or sing the alto part
examples: The orchestra auditioned several altoists for the new season.
Young altoists often look up to classic jazz players.

senses.definition: an instrument used for measuring height or elevation, especially above sea level
examples: The pilot checked the altometer before starting the descent.
Scientists used an altometer to record the mountain’s elevation.

senses.definition: having high, raised folds or frills, especially on clothing or fabric
examples: She wore an altoruffled collar that framed her face.
The dress had an altoruffled neckline and sleeves.

senses.definition: singers with low female voices, or the alto parts in music
examples: The altos came in strongly on the second verse.
The choir has three altos and two sopranos in the front row.

senses.definition: layers of gray or blue-gray mid-level clouds that often cover the whole sky
examples: Thick altostrati blocked the sun for most of the afternoon.
Pilots reported flying above several altostrati on the way north.

senses.definition: a layer of gray or blue-gray mid-level cloud that usually covers the whole sky and can bring light rain or snow
examples: A sheet of altostratus spread across the sky ahead of the warm front.
The sun appeared as a pale disk behind the altostratus.

senses.definition: a historical gold coin used in parts of the Middle East and Central Asia
examples: The museum display included a rare altoun from the sixteenth century.
Traders once paid for silk with altouns and silver pieces.

senses.definition: young birds that are born helpless and must be cared for in the nest
examples: Songbirds are typical altrices, blind and featherless at hatching.
As altrices, the chicks depended completely on their parents for food.

senses.definition: describing young animals, especially birds, that are born weak, blind, and unable to move or feed themselves
examples: Most songbirds have altricial chicks that stay in the nest for weeks.
Altricial young require intensive care from their parents.

senses.definition: young animals, especially birds, that are altricial and need extensive parental care
examples: The altricials remained in the nest while the adults searched for food.
Compared with precocials, altricials grow faster after hatching.

senses.definition: a medical treatment system that uses methods different from traditional or conventional medicine
examples: Some patients turn to altropathy when standard treatments fail.
The clinic offers altropathy alongside conventional care.

senses.definition: a rare sugar that is a stereoisomer of glucose
examples: Altrose is mainly used in research as a reference sugar.
Chemists compared the structure of altrose with other hexoses.

senses.definition: a selfless wish to help other people, even when it may bring you no benefit or even a disadvantage
examples: She donated most of her savings out of pure altruism.
Many people admire his altruism and quiet generosity.

senses.definition: behavior in animals that helps others of the same species at some cost to the animal itself
examples: In biology, altruism can explain why some animals warn others of danger.
Researchers study altruism in social insects like ants and bees.

senses.definition: acts or ideas that show a strong wish to help others more than yourself
examples: Their quiet altruisms rarely appeared in the news.
Daily altruisms, like small kindnesses, can change a community.

senses.definition: a person who cares about others and is willing to help them even at a cost to themselves
examples: Anonymously giving a kidney to a stranger is something only a true altruist might do.
He is an altruist who volunteers every weekend at the shelter.

senses.definition: showing a desire to help other people, even when it may harm or not benefit you
examples: Her decision to donate the money was completely altruistic.
Some people questioned whether his actions were truly altruistic.

senses.definition: in a way that shows a wish to help others more than yourself
examples: She acted altruistically when she offered to care for her neighbor’s children for free.
Many people donate blood altruistically, without expecting any reward.

senses.definition: people who care deeply about others and are ready to help them at a cost to themselves
examples: Altruists often give their time rather than their money.
Communities rely on altruists who volunteer in difficult situations.

senses.definition: alternative ways of doing or being, often used informally to refer to alternative accounts, identities, or styles
examples: Gamers sometimes create alts so they can play different characters.
She posts artwork on one account and keeps her alts private.

senses.definition: an old term for a kind of porcelain or china ware from Asia
examples: The collector specialized in altschin from the seventeenth century.
Altogether, the altschin vases formed the centerpiece of the display.

senses.definition: relating to the season of autumn or fall
examples: The poem is full of altumal images of falling leaves.
An altumal breeze swept through the nearly bare trees.

senses.definition: a historical gold coin formerly used in parts of the Ottoman Empire and surrounding regions
examples: Merchants counted the payment in altun before loading the goods.
An old altun was discovered during the excavation of the market site.

senses.definition: an old or dialect form of the word for height or altitude
examples: From that alture, the valley seemed very far below.
The tower’s alture gave them a view of the sea.

senses.definition: a high male voice part in early choral music, similar to the modern alto
examples: The altus in the motet carries an important inner melody.
He trained as an altus in a Renaissance vocal ensemble.

senses.definition: an informal abbreviation for aluminum, especially in technical or trade contexts
examples: The frame is made from lightweight alu.
They ordered sheets of alu for the construction project.

senses.definition: an old name for the tawny owl
examples: An aluco called from the dark woods.
The guide explained that the aluco hunts mainly at night.

senses.definition: a former scientific family name used for certain owls, now usually included in another owl family
examples: Older books place these species in Aluconidae.
Taxonomists later merged Aluconidae with a larger owl family.

senses.definition: a former subfamily name used for certain owls in older classifications
examples: The genus was once assigned to Aluconinae.
Aluconinae appears in nineteenth-century ornithological works.

senses.definition: a pear-shaped vessel open at both ends, used in a series to collect vapors during sublimation
examples: The alchemist fitted the aludel to the furnace for the experiment.
Rows of aludel pots were used to capture the metal vapors.

senses.definition: pear-shaped vessels open at both ends, set in a series to collect vapors during sublimation
examples: Several aludels were stacked to form a long condenser.
The old laboratory still displayed its original aludels.

senses.definition: a bright star in the constellation Canis Major
examples: Aludra forms part of the tail of the big dog constellation.
Astronomers observed changes in the brightness of Aludra.

senses.definition: a small group of feathers on a bird’s wing, attached to the first digit, that helps control airflow in flight
examples: The bird spread its alula to slow down before landing.
Damage to the alula can make flying more difficult.

senses.definition: the small groups of feathers on the wings of birds that help with control during flight
examples: Many birds lift their alulae when they land.
The researcher compared the alulae of different species.

senses.definition: relating to the alula of a bird
examples: The alular feathers were shorter in this species.
Alular movements help the bird control its speed in flight.

senses.definition: small groups of feathers on birds’ wings that act like little flaps to help during slow flight or landing
examples: Hawks raise their alulas when they glide slowly.
The biologist carefully measured the alulas of the specimen.

senses.definition: a small alula or a small flap-like feather structure on a bird’s wing
examples: Tiny alulets appeared along the wing margin.
The fossil showed an alulet near the leading edge of the wing.

senses.definition: a legendary early king mentioned in ancient Mesopotamian lists of rulers
examples: Alulim appears in old Sumerian king lists as a primeval ruler.
Stories about Alulim blend myth with early history.

senses.definition: a double sulfate salt used in industry and medicine, for example to clean water or stop bleeding
examples: They added alum to the water as a cleaning agent.
He pressed an alum block against the small cut after shaving.

senses.definition: a former student of a school or college, especially in North American English
examples: She is an alum of the university’s law program.
The event was organized by a group of proud alums.

senses.definition: a crust or coating of alum that forms on a surface, especially in old industrial settings
examples: Alumbloom covered the inside of the old vat.
The workers scraped the alumbloom from the walls of the tank.

senses.definition: a member of a Spanish religious movement in the sixteenth century that stressed inner spiritual light
examples: The Inquisition tried several alumbrado leaders.
Historians study alumbrados to understand early mystic movements.

senses.definition: a nickel-based alloy used in thermocouples for measuring temperature
examples: The engineer selected alumel for the thermocouple wire.
Alumel and chromel are paired in common temperature sensors.

senses.definition: an old or Latin name for alum, a double sulfate used in medicine and industry
examples: The recipe called for alumen to fix the dye.
In early texts, alumen often refers to potassium alum.

senses.definition: to treat or coat something with alum or an alum compound
examples: They alumetized the fabric to help the dye last longer.
The company plans to alumetize the paper for special printing.

senses.definition: relating to or containing alum
examples: The rock showed alumian layers near the surface.
Alumian deposits colored the water a pale white.

senses.definition: relating to aluminum or aluminum compounds
examples: Alumic salts were used in the experiment.
The alumic content of the ore was measured carefully.

senses.definition: containing alum or alum-producing minerals
examples: The region is known for its alumiferous shales.
Alumiferous rocks were mined for use in dyeing cloth.
